Monoculture bacteria strain in 0 hr and 24 hr (OD1)

Compile data from different sheets to get population size at start (0 hr) and end (24 hr) points under OD = 1.

OD0.1 and OD0.5 data reconstruction and binding to OD1 data

Based on May 7th Discussion, conducted monoculture growth assay at lower initial densities. Compile data from all OD values.

Monogrowth line in different OD level

For each microbe, we show the population size change over 24 hours. Colors represent different starting OD values.

\(\alpha_{ii}\) and \(r_i\) from monoculture (statistical fitting)

Consider a Lotkta–Volterra competition model in the form of \(\frac{dN_i}{dt} = N_i(r_i-\alpha_{ii}N_i-\alpha_{ij}N_j)\). Here, we combined all monoculture growth assay data from different initial OD values to perform a linear regression (all density data were scaled by \(10^9\) prior to calculation). The response variable is \(\frac{\Delta lnN_i}{\Delta t}\), which is an estimate of the species per capita growth rate, and the dependent variable is the initial density at 0 hr. This allowed us to estimate each species’ intraspecific competition: the y-intercept represents the species’ intrinsic growth rate (\(r_i\)) and the slope represents the strength of intraspecific competition (\(\alpha_{ii}\)). We were able to employ this statistical approach because the different OD dataset spans a large-enough range of initial values. Below, we show: (1) a depiction of the statistial fit, (2) species’ estimated intraspecific competition strength \(\alpha_{ii}\), and (3) species’ estimated intrinsic growth rate \(r_i\). Microbe JUb19 is a species with high intrinsic growth rate and weak self-limitation.
